description: Structural classification of erotic poses and sex positions as they appear in adult video and print. Covers the position family (solo / one-on-one / group), the primary act, the partner arrangement, penetration type, facing, dynamics, and the surface or support used. The arrangement sub-groups follow the widely-used structural taxonomy of named positions (penetrating-partner-on-top, from-behind, receiving-partner-on-top, seated/kneeling, standing, side-lying, inverted) documented in reference works such as the Wikipedia 'List of sex positions' and Wikidata's 'sex position' (Q8394) class; all bucket definitions here are original wording. Reference-grade descriptors intended for content tagging, search facets, and text-to-image prompt conditioning. Adults only.

dimensions.scale_citation: Top-level partition by participant count, aligned with the group-vs-pair distinction used across sexology reference works.
dimensions.description: Highest-level structural grouping by number of participants.

values.definition: A single participant; self-stimulation or display posing.
Two participants.
Three or more participants.

dimensions.scale_citation: Aligned with the exclusively-penetrative / non-exclusively-penetrative / non-penetrative partition used in named-position reference taxonomies.
dimensions.description: The dominant sexual act that defines the position. A position may involve more than one act; this records the defining one.

values.definition: Penile or object vaginal penetration is the defining act.
Penile or object anal penetration is the defining act.
Oral-genital or oral-anal contact is the defining act.
Manual stimulation (hand/finger) is the defining act.
Body-to-body contact without penetration (e.g. frottage, intercrural, tribadism).
Solo posing or self-stimulation for the camera; no partner contact.
Multiple simultaneous acts of comparable prominence (common in group positions).

dimensions.scale_citation: Structural sub-groups mirror the named-position organisation in the Wikipedia 'List of sex positions' (penetrating-on-top, from-behind, receiving-on-top, sitting/kneeling, standing, less-common/inverted); wording original.
dimensions.description: The spatial arrangement of the participants relative to one another.

values.definition: Penetrating partner above the receiving partner (e.g. missionary family).
Receiving partner astride or above (e.g. cowgirl / woman-on-top family).
Penetration or contact from the rear (e.g. doggy style, prone bone, spoons).
One or both partners seated (e.g. lotus, lap position).
One or both partners kneeling.
One or both partners standing, possibly with support.
Both partners on their sides (e.g. spoons, lateral).
One partner partly or fully inverted (e.g. piledriver, suspended).
Reciprocal, oriented head-to-toe (e.g. 69).
One partner positioned above the other's face (e.g. facesitting).
Single participant; no partner arrangement.

dimensions.scale_citation: Descriptive enumeration of penetration configurations, including the multi-penetration terms catalogued on Wikidata.
dimensions.description: Which orifices are penetrated and by how many partners, where applicable.

values.definition: No penetration.
Single vaginal penetration.
Single anal penetration.
Oral penetration.
Hand or finger insertion.
Two simultaneous vaginal penetrations.
Two simultaneous anal penetrations.
Simultaneous vaginal and anal penetration by different partners.
Three or more simultaneous penetrations, or a combination not otherwise listed.

dimensions.scale_citation: Relative orientation descriptor used in position taxonomies.
dimensions.description: Orientation of participants relative to each other.

values.definition: Partners face each other.
One partner's front to the other's back.
Reversed, reciprocal orientation.
Partners aligned laterally.
Orientation varies or is not fixed.

dimensions.scale_citation: Motion descriptor for distinguishing static hold poses from active positions in moving-image media.
dimensions.description: The characteristic motion of the position, relevant to video vs. still-image depiction.

values.definition: A held pose with little movement; typical of print stills.
Reciprocating penetrative motion.
Circular or rocking contact motion.
Receiving partner drives vertical motion from above.
Rhythmic oral motion.

observability.notes: Not assessable from a single still; applies to moving-image sources.

dimensions.scale_citation: Aligned with the 'furniture and apparatus' distinctions drawn in named-position references.
dimensions.description: The surface or means of support the position relies on.

values.definition: Horizontal surface such as a bed or floor.
One partner at the edge of a bed or table.
Wall used for bracing, usually standing.
Standing without external bracing.
Chair, couch, or similar.
One partner bears the other's weight (e.g. carried).
One partner suspended or largely off any surface.
Purpose-built furniture or apparatus is central to the position.

aggregation_notes.note: This vocabulary describes named positions, not observed image samples; roll-ups apply when tagging a corpus against it.

references: Wikidata. Sex position (Q8394). https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q8394 (structured data CC0).
Wikipedia contributors. List of sex positions. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_sex_positions (structure referenced; wording original).
Vatsyayana. Kama Sutra (public domain) — historical named-position source.
Danbooru tag group: posture and sex_acts — folksonomy structure for position and participant-composition axes.
